title: mechanical
main_content:
Tumour
cells invading into the TME showed elongated nuclei compared with
the bulk tumour mass (Fig. 1j,k).
context:
A previous study showed that tumour
nuclei become highly elongated when squeezing through mechanically
restrictive environments12. Although numerous factors probably influ-
ence tumour invasion,
outcome:
we hypothesized that mechanical forces exerted
on the cell/nucleus may cause stable changes in gene expression and
tumour cell behaviour.
predicted_context: Consistent with the biomechanics of 3D cell migration, it is well established that extreme nuclear deformation, specifically nuclear elongation, is a primary morphological hallmark of cells undergoing physical constriction and mechanical confinement as they squeeze through restrictive pores in dense extracellular matrices.
predicted_outcome: Therefore, this suggests that the local architecture of the tumour microenvironment imposes substantial mechanical confinement on invading melanoma cells, forcing a physical compression of the nucleus that serves as a mechanical cue to alter chromatin architecture and drive the phenotypic transition into an invasive state.

title: mechanical
main_content:
These results indicate
that confined melanoma cells rapidly undergo structural changes in
the MT cytoskeleton resembling neurons.
context:
Acetylated tubulin mediates neuronal architecture and function
and marks axonal MTs18. Tubulin acetylation stabilizes MTs and thus
the cell against mechanical pressure19. Curved MTs, such as those we
observed in confined cells (Fig. 2a,b), are also indicative of long-lived,
stabilized MTs19.
outcome:
We performed immunofluorescence on sections from
adult fish with transgenic BRAFV600E-driven melanomas ... Confining A375 cells in vitro
predicted_context: Melanoma cells, owing to their neural crest developmental origin, exhibit significant phenotypic plasticity and can adopt neural-like morphologies under microenvironmental stress. Microtubules (MTs) are the primary structural determinants of neurite-like extensions. Utilizing engineered microenvironments, such as polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) microchannels, is a highly established method to apply standardized mechanical confinement to tumor cells in vitro to study subsequent spatial and cytoskeletal adaptations.
predicted_outcome: Seed melanoma cells into PDMS microfluidic channels of varying widths (e.g., 3 μm for severe confinement vs. 50 μm for unconfined controls), fix the cells, and perform immunofluorescence staining using antibodies against alpha-tubulin and the neuronal microtubule marker beta-III tubulin (TUBB3). Subsequently, use high-resolution confocal microscopy to visualize the MT architecture and quantify the formation of neurite-like structural protrusions.

title: mechanical
main_content:
However, most MTs in confined and
unconfined A375 cells were highly tyrosinated (Extended Data Fig. 4a–d), ...
Although confined perinuclear MTs were occasionally
tyrosinated and rarely detyrosinated (Extended Data Fig. 4a–d), there
was no specific enrichment of these PTMs in the perinuclear network,
as observed for acetylated MTs. Similar results were observed for poly-
glutamylated MTs (Extended Data Fig. 4e,f). No polyglycylated MTs
were found in either condition (Extended Data Fig. 4g,h).
context:
Detyrosination
has been linked to stabilized MTs26 ... probably owing to the high concentrations of tyrosine and tyrosinase in melanoma27
outcome:
This indicates
that acetylation is the primary PTM mediating the assembly and/or
stability of the perinuclear tubulin network.
predicted_context: It is well established that the 'tubulin code'—comprising distinct microtubule post-translational modifications (PTMs)—differentially regulates microtubule mechanics and functions. While modifications like tyrosination generally mark dynamic, newly formed microtubules, alpha-tubulin acetylation uniquely marks long-lived microtubules and confers specific structural flexibility, protecting the microtubule lattice against mechanical stress and deformation.
predicted_outcome: This indicates that the adaptation of melanoma cells to mechanical confinement does not involve a global or non-specific alteration of microtubule dynamics, but rather relies on the highly selective enrichment of microtubule acetylation to specifically stabilize the perinuclear network against environmental mechanical stress.

title: mechanical
main_content:
Homer de novo motif analysis
identified conserved transcription factor binding motifs enriched within the promoter of open chromatin regions upon HMGB2 overex-
pression (HMGB2OE). The top-ranked motif was an AP-1 motif (Fig. 4k;
P = 1 × 10−49) ...
Other highly ranked motifs included PRDM4, ... (Fig. 4k; P = 1 × 10−12), and SOX9, ... (Fig. 4k; P = 1 × 10−11).
context:
...implicated in melanoma and melanocyte plasticity40,41... which functions in neural
development42 ... a critical regulator of
the pro-invasive phenotype switch in melanoma43
outcome:
These data indicate that HMGB2 upregulation increases chromatin
accessibility at loci associated with plasticity and a neuronal pheno-
type, promoting a confinement-induced dedifferentiation program.
predicted_context: It is well established that the transcription factors AP-1 and SOX9 act as master regulators of phenotype switching in melanoma, specifically driving the gene expression programs required for the transition from a melanocytic, proliferative state to a dedifferentiated, highly invasive, and mesenchymal-like state.
predicted_outcome: Therefore, this indicates that HMGB2 acts as an upstream epigenetic driver of melanoma plasticity by remodeling chromatin architecture to increase the accessibility of promoters for AP-1 and SOX9, thereby facilitating the active transcriptional rewiring necessary for melanoma cells to transition into an invasive state.

title: mechanical
outcome:
we performed chromatin immu-
noprecipitation (ChIP) sequencing (ChIP–seq) targeting HMGB2 in
A375 cells using a double-crosslinking approach44 (Extended Data
Fig. 11a). We generated a peak atlas by comparing peaks present in cells
expressing baseline concentrations of HMGB2 to those not present in
HMGB2KO cells
predicted_context: High Mobility Group Box 2 (HMGB2) is a non-histone chromatin architectural protein that dynamically interacts with DNA to remodel chromatin landscape and regulate gene transcription. Chromatin immunoprecipitation followed by high-throughput sequencing (ChIP-seq) is the established, gold-standard methodology previously used to successfully map the genome-wide DNA-binding sites and direct downstream gene targets of HMGB2 in various cellular contexts.
predicted_outcome: We will perform Chromatin Immunoprecipitation sequencing (ChIP-seq) using an anti-HMGB2 antibody on the melanoma cell lines, followed by peak calling and motif analysis to map its genome-wide binding sites and identify direct transcriptional targets.

title: mechanical
subsection: HMGB2 drives invasion through Notch and BRN2
main_content:
HMGB2 robustly
bound the promoter of Notch signalling genes in both A375 (Extended
Data Fig. 11d and Supplementary Table 6) and SKMEL5 (Extended Data
Fig. 12d and Supplementary Table 8) cells. Notch family genes were also
upregulated by human interface cells (DLL1, DLL3 and DLK2; Supple-
mentary Table 1) and confined human melanoma cells (NOTCH2NLA,
DLK2 and DLL4; Supplementary Table 2).
context:
Notch signalling promotes
melanoma invasion through brain-2 (BRN2, also known as POU3F2)
(ref. 48), a well-characterized pro-invasive transcription factor in mela-
noma49 that also functions in neuronal development50.
outcome:
This indicates that invasive and neuronal phenotypes in confined interface cells are
induced by HMGB2-mediated upregulation of Notch/BRN2 signal-
ling.
predicted_context: It is well established that the chromatin architectural protein HMGB2 facilitates transcription by altering DNA conformation to enhance transcription factor binding, and that the upregulation of the Notch signaling pathway plays a critical role in driving melanoma progression, epithelial-to-mesenchymal-like transitions, and invasive cellular plasticity.
predicted_outcome: Therefore, mechanical confinement induces HMGB2-dependent chromatin remodeling at Notch signaling promoters, driving the transcriptional activation of the Notch pathway which mechanistically facilitates the phenotypic transition of melanoma cells from a proliferative to an invasive state.

title: taurine
main_content:
To define temporal changes in non-immune bone marrow micro-
environment populations during myeloid leukaemia progression
context: which can grow in unirradiated mouse recipients
outcome:
we used bcCML LSCs...(Extended Data Fig. 1a,b). Bone marrow stromal populations
(leukaemia−CD45−TER119−) were isolated from leukaemic cohorts at
distinct stages of disease progression and analysed using scRNA-seq
(Fig. 1a).
predicted_context: Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) is an established and powerful method for dissecting cellular heterogeneity and tracking dynamic transcriptional remodeling within the tumor microenvironment over time. Previous studies have demonstrated that selecting for CD45-negative (non-immune) cells from the bone marrow, followed by scRNA-seq, allows for the high-resolution mapping of the bone marrow stroma taxonomy and the identification of distinct, disease-driven alterations in niche populations such as mesenchymal stromal cells, osteoblasts, and endothelial cells.
predicted_outcome: Isolate CD45-negative (non-immune) bone marrow cells using fluorescence-activated cell sorting (FACS) from a myeloid leukaemia mouse model (e.g., MLL-AF9) at multiple longitudinal time points (such as healthy baseline, intermediate pre-leukaemic, and terminal advanced stages), and perform single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) to chart the dynamic subpopulation shifts and transcriptomic changes.

title: taurine
main_content: This identified 13 cell surface signals common to both bcCML and AML, 18 unique to AML and 7 unique to bcCML. Of these 38 genes, 16 were misannotated as cell surface in the reference dataset19 and were removed from further analysis (Methods and Extended Data Fig. 2c,d). ... to identify ligands for these receptors,
context: especially those that were significantly upregulated in our TME scRNA-seq data and in the human AML immune microenvironment20
outcome: We used NicheNet and the published literature ... (Extended Data Fig. 2e).
predicted_context: Intercellular communication between the tumor microenvironment (TME) and cancer cells can be systematically inferred from single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) data using curated computational databases of known ligand-receptor protein complexes, such as CellPhoneDB or the FANTOM5 interaction network. These bioinformatic tools map receptor expression profiles on target cells to ligand expression profiles in surrounding stromal or immune populations, establishing putative paracrine signaling networks.
predicted_outcome: We queried the remaining 22 validated LSC-specific cell surface receptors against curated ligand-receptor interaction databases and mapped them to our bone marrow microenvironment scRNA-seq dataset to determine which distinct TME cell populations express their cognate ligands.

title: taurine
main_content: Consistent with a functional role of taurine from the TME in leukaemia progression, taurine levels were 1.7-fold higher in leukaemic bone marrow interstitial fluid as compared to the controls (Fig. 2j).
context: In mice, the majority of taurine is synthesized in the liver from cysteine24.
outcome: To determine whether taurine produced locally in the bone marrow niche is essential for leukaemia growth,
predicted_context: It is well established that the tumor microenvironment (TME) undergoes dynamic metabolic reprogramming, resulting in the localized accumulation of specific metabolites in extracellular spaces to support cancer cell survival, bioenergetics, and disease progression. Furthermore, elevated extracellular taurine availability and increased expression of its transporter (SLC6A6/TAUT) have been previously associated with enhanced cellular survival, stress resistance, and tumor progression in various solid and hematological malignancies.
predicted_outcome: Therefore, this indicates that the bone marrow niche is actively remodeled during leukemia progression to establish a taurine-enriched metabolic microenvironment. This extracellular reservoir provides an abundant supply of taurine that can be imported by leukemic cells to drive metabolic adaptations, such as upregulated glycolysis, thereby fueling leukemogenesis and promoting aggressive disease expansion.

title: taurine
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: To determine whether small-molecule inhibitors of TAUT can effectively block leukaemia growth,
outcome: we used two well-characterized structural analogues of taurine that inhibit uptake: 6-aminomethyl3-methyl-4H-1,2,4-benzothiadiazine-1,1-dioxide hydrochloride35 (TAG) and guanidinoethyl sulphonate36 (GES; Extended Data Fig.8d,e).
predicted_context: Guanidinoethyl sulfonate (GES) is a well-established, competitive small-molecule inhibitor of the taurine transporter (TAUT/SLC6A6) that effectively restricts intracellular taurine uptake. Evaluating the pre-clinical efficacy of metabolic inhibitors in leukemia standardly requires testing these compounds in vivo using patient-derived xenograft (PDX) or syngeneic mouse models to accurately measure their impact on tumor microenvironment interactions, disease progression, and overall survival.
predicted_outcome: Treat human AML cell lines and murine leukemia models (such as patient-derived xenografts) with the TAUT inhibitor guanidinoethyl sulfonate (GES). Subsequently, measure in vitro cell proliferation and apoptosis, quantify in vivo leukemic blast burden in the bone marrow and spleen using flow cytometry, and track the overall survival curves of the mice compared to vehicle-treated controls.

title: taurine
main_content: Our untargeted metabolomic approach identified 13C label only in known taurine conjugates, N-acetyl taurine and glutaurine (Extended Data Fig.10f,g). Membrane-permeable taurine fully rescued colonies formed by Slc6a6−/− cells (Fig. 5g), and N-acetyl-taurine partly rescued this defect (Extended Data Fig.10h), ...  By contrast, glutaurine was unable to rescue the Slc6a6−/− colony formation (Extended Data Fig.10i).
context: perhaps by breakdown to taurine and acetate37.
outcome: These experiments indicate that taurine, and not a secondary metabolite, promotes leukaemia growth due to an indirect effect on glycolysis, possibly through downstream signalling.
predicted_context: It is well established that the SLC6A6 gene encodes the primary taurine transporter (TAUT), which facilitates the essential cellular uptake of taurine from the microenvironment. Once inside the cell, free taurine can be enzymatically converted into downstream conjugates, such as N-acetyl taurine and glutaurine, which serve distinct physiological functions, act as metabolic reservoirs, or facilitate excretion, but often exhibit different bioactivity profiles compared to the free amino acid.
predicted_outcome: This indicates that free, unconjugated taurine is the primary biologically active metabolite required to drive leukaemic cell survival and colony formation, while its downstream conjugates are either physiologically inactive or functionally insufficient to independently promote leukaemogenesis.

title: taurine
main_content: ... mTOR signalling has a key role downstream of taurine in leukaemia cells.
context: Amino acid availability can be sensed by RAG GTPases to facilitate mTORC1 localization to the outer lysosomal membrane40 and its interaction with RHEB GTPases41, thereby promoting mTORC1 activation.
outcome: We hypothesized that taurine may activate mTOR by promoting its interaction with RAG GTPases.
predicted_context: Canonical mTORC1 activation is tightly regulated by specific proteogenic amino acids (such as leucine and arginine) via Rag GTPases, whereas taurine is a non-proteogenic amino sulfonic acid not traditionally known to engage these classical nutrient sensors. Additionally, direct therapeutic targeting of mTOR in AML frequently induces harmful compensatory survival networks, notably PI3K/AKT hyperactivation, which limits the clinical efficacy of isolated mTOR inhibition.
predicted_outcome: Does taurine-induced mTOR activation rely on non-canonical, Rag GTPase-independent signaling mechanisms, and does therapeutically blocking upstream taurine uptake via TAUT successfully bypass the compensatory PI3K/AKT feedback loops normally triggered by direct mTOR inhibitors in leukaemia?

title: breast_cancer
main_content: A germline deletion involving APOBEC3A and APOBEC3B (APOBEC3A/B), which effectively generates APOBEC3AAPOBEC3B fusion transcripts ... the deletion was not substantially enriched in patients with breast cancer compared with the background Korean population (51.0%, 844 out of 1,654 individuals, with 150 being homozygous and 694 heterozygous; P = 0.276 by two-sided chi-square test; Fig. 2g),
context: A germline deletion involving APOBEC3A and APOBEC3B (APOBEC3A/B), which effectively generates APOBEC3AAPOBEC3B fusion transcripts, has been suggested to predispose to APOBEC-mediated hypermutations29 (Extended Data Fig.4a).... Contrary to previous reports30,31,
outcome: suggesting that it is not a strong cancer-predisposing germline variant.
predicted_context: The APOBEC3A/B germline deletion is a well-characterized polymorphism that is known to vary significantly across different ethnic groups; it is highly prevalent in East Asian and Oceanian populations but relatively rare in populations of European descent. It is well established that this deletion can drive APOBEC-associated hypermutation signatures in various cancers and has been suggested by previous studies to act as a breast cancer susceptibility allele in certain cohorts.
predicted_outcome: Therefore, this suggests that despite its potential to influence the mutational landscape of a tumor once it develops, the APOBEC3A/B germline deletion does not independently confer a significantly elevated risk for breast cancer onset in the Korean population. Because it exists as a highly frequent background polymorphism in this demographic, it cannot be reliably used as a population-wide predictive biomarker for breast cancer susceptibility in East Asian cohorts.

title: breast_cancer
main_content: To determine the structural variation landscape,
context: A key mutational process in cancer is structural variation, which acts to amplify, delete or reorder chromosomal material at scales that range from single genes to entire chromosomes32
outcome: we generated a DNA interaction map between 5-Mb segments across the entire genome (Fig. 3a), along with large-scale copy number amplification patterns (CNAs; G-score).
predicted_context: Traditional targeted sequencing approaches primarily focus on individual point mutations in known coding genes and frequently miss large-scale structural changes, such as non-coding genomic rearrangements and copy number alterations. Whole-genome sequencing (WGS) provides an unbiased, comprehensive view of the entire genome, making it the established gold-standard methodology for detecting the full spectrum of structural variations and complex chromosomal rearrangements in cancer genomes.
predicted_outcome: Perform whole-genome sequencing (WGS) on tumor tissues and matched normal samples from the 1,364 breast cancer cohort, and apply structural variant and copy-number alteration calling algorithms to map genomic rearrangements across the entire genome.

title: breast_cancer
main_content: Another frequent interchromosomal translocation involved the ERBB2 locus on chromosome 17 and a locus on chromosome 20 (observed in 30 participants), ... (Fig. 3a, top right). ... structural variations involving the ERBB2 locus were clustered with super-enhancers genome-wide (P = 0.028 by permutation test; Fig. 3b), which translocated super-enhancers in about 90 kb from the ERBB2 locus (Fig. 3c). Tumours with extragenic structural variants in the vicinity (within 1 Mb) showed significantly higher ERBB2 gene expression levels than their gene copy number (log2 fold change >1, q-value <0.10; Fig. 3d). They often carried extragenic structural variants in the vicinity (within 1 Mb) with a cancer cell fraction (CCF) of approximately 1 (Fig. 3e), and distal breakpoints significantly enriched near breast cancer-specific super-enhancers35 (Fig. 3f).
context: where breast cancer super-enhancers were enriched35 ... Structural variations can influence  transcription by disrupting enhancer–promoter interactions, chromatin topology and long-range regulatory elements36. In line with this idea,
outcome: Our observation overall suggests a possible enhancer-hijacking mechanism for transcriptional activation in breast cancer genomes.
predicted_context: It is well established that genomic structural variations can spatially reposition highly active, distal cis-regulatory elements—such as super-enhancers—into the close proximity of oncogenes. This phenomenon, known as 'enhancer hijacking', drives massive and aberrant transcriptional activation of the juxtaposed target genes, promoting oncogenesis independently of localized gene amplification or focal copy number gains.
predicted_outcome: Therefore, this indicates that structural variant-mediated enhancer hijacking serves as a distinct, alternative oncogenic mechanism for ERBB2 (HER2) hyperactivation in breast cancer. Furthermore, because these translocations are present at a cancer cell fraction of approximately 1, the repositioning of super-enhancers to the ERBB2 locus constitutes an early, clonal driver event in the evolutionary trajectory of these specific tumors.

title: breast_cancer
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Out of 75 individuals with HER2-positive tumours who received neoadjuvant TCHP regimens (docetaxel (microtubule inhibitor), carboplatin (platinum intercalator), trastuzumab and pertuzumab (anti-HER2 inhibitors)), 38 showed pathologic complete response (pCR) (Fig. 4e and Supplementary Table 4). … cases with pCR (n = 38) exhibited significantly higher ERBB2 expression (P < 0.001, two-sided Student’s t-test) and a greater frequency of ERBB2 focal amplification (P = 0.001, two-sided Fisher’s exact test; Extended Data Fig.9f–h (external validation in the TransNEO cohort46)).  ... Notably, in our cohort, tumours with ERBB2  copy number of 33 or more were significantly more likely to be ecDNA-positive (P = 0.003; Fig. 4f, right); however, ERBB2 amplification by ecDNA was not associated with pCR rates (P = 0.540).
context: Of note, ecDNA is generally associated with poor prognosis across multiple cancer types47.
outcome: Our observation suggests that absolute copy number. rather than the amplification mechanism, may be a more relevant predictor of therapeutic response. It also indicates that ERBB2-amplifying ecDNA may be an actionable target for anti-HER2 therapy.
predicted_context: It is a well-established principle that elevated ERBB2 (HER2) focal amplification and overexpression predict favorable clinical responses to anti-HER2 targeted therapies. The observation that higher ERBB2 expression aligns with higher pathologic complete response (pCR) rates is highly consistent with standard clinical biomarkers. Furthermore, it is known that extrachromosomal DNA (ecDNA) facilitates massive oncogene copy number amplification. However, because ecDNA lacks centromeres and segregates unequally during mitosis, it rapidly drives extreme intratumoral heterogeneity, genomic plasticity, and targeted therapy resistance.
predicted_outcome: This suggests that while stable chromosomal ERBB2 amplification confers vulnerability to HER2-targeted neoadjuvant therapy, the structural modality of the amplification alters therapeutic susceptibility. Specifically, ecDNA-mediated ERBB2 amplification fails to proportionally improve treatment response despite driving extreme copy numbers, indicating that the rapid, dynamic intratumoral heterogeneity and unequal segregation conferred by ecDNA provide a mechanism for tumor sub-clones to escape targeted inhibition, thereby mitigating the therapeutic efficacy of the TCHP regimen.

title: breast_cancer
main_content: The timing analysis with the burden of co-amplified, pre-amplification somatic base substitutions yielded the acquisition timing of the frequent long-segmental CNAs in breast cancer in molecular time49 (Extended Data Fig. 10). Most of the recurrent long-segmental CNAs were acquired by 20% of molecular time, when recent common ancestral cells of a cancer emerge, which should be decades earlier than tumour diagnosis.
context: which is presumably acquired in early puberty, consistent with a previous report50
outcome: This implies that acquisition of the long-segmental CNAs is an early evolutionary event in breast cancer, which is presumably acquired in early puberty ... Our findings also suggest that full neoplastic transformation can take decades from the first event of genomic instability.
predicted_context: These findings are highly consistent with pan-cancer evolutionary timeline studies demonstrating that large-scale structural variations, including chromosomal gains and extensive copy number alterations (CNAs), frequently occur as early initiating events in a tumor's life history. It is a well-established biological paradigm in cancer genomics that such macro-evolutionary leaps establish the foundational genomic architecture of the tumor and drive initial clonal expansion, often preceding clinical presentation by many years.
predicted_outcome: This indicates that the fundamental structural framework and critical chromosomal instabilities characterizing these breast cancers are locked in during the nascent stages of oncogenesis, long before clinically evident malignant transformation. Consequently, these recurrent long-segmental CNAs act as very early driver events, revealing a prolonged subclinical evolutionary phase that could offer a substantial window of opportunity for early detection and interception strategies targeting these ancestral clones.
